prompt: Show one family photo with the date, who is in it, and a private caption only I can edit.
Make a browsable family photo album with filters for year and person.
Build the shared family photo wall everyone in the house can browse and react to.
Show one grocery item with quantity, store, and whether we already bought it.
Create the shared grocery list for this week and let us sort it by aisle or store.
Build the home dashboard block with the shared calendar, grocery list, and chore chart visible in one place.
Store my personal journal entries where nobody else can see them.
Create a shared family photo album that everyone in the house can add to.
Build a household dashboard — shared calendar, grocery list, chore chart — but each person also has their own private space.
I use Telegram for my hobby group and WhatsApp for family. Show me both in one inbox but keep them visually separated.
I've been using my recipe collection privately. Now I want to share new recipes automatically to my Telegram cooking group whenever I add one.
batch name, brew date, recipe reference, beer/wine style, original gravity

Bounded utility module that measures soft-wrapped line count in constrained-width text containers. Input: plain text string, container width in pixels, optional style object (font-family, font-size, line-height, letter-spacing). Output: line count as integer, or full metrics object with line count, calculated total height, overflow indicator. Implementation uses invisible buffer element technique to mirror styles and measure actual rendered dimensions. Must handle: newline characters, trailing whitespace, varying character widths, CSS whitespace normalization. Must NOT include: text editing, formatting, rich text, network calls, persistent storage.
Targeted threat remediation utility focused on one specific malware family

Create a bounded sovereign module for a personal dessert recipe organizer. Key features: categorized browsing (Cakes, Cheesecakes, Cookies, Muffins, Pies, Quickbreads, Squares, Various), recipe detail with tips/comments/quotes, kitchen notes (Well-Stocked Kitchen), print-friendly formatting, custom recipe entry. Local storage only, offline-first, single user. Include view logic and data schema hints.

Create a bounded recipe organizer that stores manual recipe entries, lets users select which recipes to cook, and automatically generates a consolidated shopping list organized by store aisle. Local-first with no cloud sync required. No web recipe scraping - manual entry only.

A user wants to: enter a recipe with ingredient names and quantities, retrieve or local-cache nutritional data for each ingredient from a food database, calculate total and per-serving nutrients (calories, macros, micros), compare two recipes or two ingredients side-by-side, and export a nutrition report as table or chart.

text (string), container (width, height in pixels or CSS selector reference), typography (initial font family, font size, style flags for bold/extended), fitting rules (step-down increment, whether to reduce style weight as fallback, minimum size constraint)
A home cook wants to store and organize their personal recipe collection with full offline access, quick search, and easy retrieval while cooking
system|role=garden-journal-keeper|context=personal home garden tracking|location=local-first|subject=garden-activities-plant-records
A homebrewer needs to calculate the expected IBU bitterness for a beer recipe with multiple hop additions at different boil times.

You are building a bounded personal-finance module for a local-first household portfolio and net-worth tracker. The module should allow users to: (1) register multiple investment accounts with manual entry or CSV import, (2) maintain a holdings ledger with cost basis and purchase dates, (3) update position valuations, (4) calculate realized/unrealized capital gains using FIFO or LIFO methods, (5) compute aggregate net worth across accounts, (6) track dividend income. Prioritize local data storage and privacy. Describe the module scope, key entities, and primary flows.

Create a local-first desktop genealogy application called 'Heritage Keeper' that helps users compile and organize family history records. The application should store person records locally with fields for name, dates, places, biography, and custom attributes. It must support creating family relationships (parents, spouses, siblings) to form tree structures. Include source documentation with citation builder and evidence rating. The system should handle photo and document attachments linked to individuals. Provide full-text search across all records. Generate printable reports including family group sheets, ancestor/descendant trees, timelines, and birthday calendars. Support GEDCOM import/export for data portability. The UI should offer both list/browser views and tree visualization. All data stays on the user's machine—no cloud dependency.

A home cook wants to save recipes they find online or in cookbooks, organize them by category and tags, and quickly find the right recipe when cooking. They want offline access and no cloud dependency.

child age|school type preference|in-state or out-of-state|preferred institution type|years until enrollment|current household income|current college savings|monthly contribution capacity|preferred savings vehicle

Design a bounded Greek alphabet learning module as a standalone sovereign module. Include: (1) reference card viewer showing Greek letters with uppercase, lowercase, and transliteration; (2) flashcard study mode with flip interaction; (3) quiz mode with multiple choice and type-the-answer formats testing letter recognition; (4) spaced repetition scheduling for optimal review timing; (5) session-based progress tracking showing mastery per letter. The module should work offline/local-first, store progress locally, and present one letter family (alpha-epsilon, etc.) at a time during study.

You are building Kitchen Canvas, a bounded local-first recipe browser and collection module. Design the module structure, core workflows for browsing and selecting recipes by category and skill level, the preview-to-purchase flow mirroring the original demo model, and the minimal data schema for recipe storage and scaling.

Design a bounded personal nutrition tracking module that enables users to log food intake, analyze nutrient consumption against personalized daily targets, compare nutritional profiles between foods, and visualize nutrient intake trends over time. The module should include a food database with nutrient information, meal logging functionality, automatic per-user RDA adjustments, food comparison views, time-series tracking with charts, and tabular reporting. Include an exchange group calculator for diabetic meal planning. Prioritize local-first data ownership with optional sync. The module should be self-contained and bounded to nutrition tracking only.

Design and implement a single-module recipe management application that runs entirely offline on the user's device. The app stores recipes locally (JSON file or SQLite) with these required fields per recipe: (1) dish name, (2) description/notes, (3) ingredients list, (4) cooking method and tools. Include: a list view showing all saved recipes, a detail/view mode, an edit form, and a print-friendly card view that renders one recipe cleanly for physical printing or PDF export. No account, no cloud sync, no social features. The UX should prioritize ease of data entry and reliable printed output.
User wants to create a personalized animated holiday greeting card to send to friends and family

Design a personal journal module that enables users to record annual camp or trip experiences across multiple years. Each entry should support text notes, linked photos, videos, and related artifacts. Include features for marking milestones, tracking preparation activities, logging friendships formed, and reviewing past entries on a timeline. Prioritize offline-first local storage with optional cloud backup. The module should support yearly organization with easy navigation between entries and provide gentle reflection prompts for periodic review.

A blank personal recipe card template for local use. Users manually fill in recipe name, ingredients list, cooking instructions, prep/cook time, servings, category, and personal notes. Template displays one recipe per card view. Export option to print on standard index card size (4x6 or 5x7) for physical recipe box or binder. No cloud storage, no social sharing, no automated recipe import - just a simple blank form for handwritten or typed personal recipe entry. Include basic instructions for how to use as a personal recipe collection.

You are a bounded local-first recipe catalog module. You store homebrew recipes as structured records, provide searchable views, support marking recipes for batch printing, and render printable brew sheet views (1 or 2 per page). Do not expand into recipe design calculators, inventory management, batch logging, or cloud sharing. Stay focused on catalog + find + mark + print.

A local-first genealogy tree builder with typed relationship records, photo attachments, contextual help, and PDF/report export capabilities. Support adding root person, then creating parent/child/sibling/spouse linked records. Include image attachment per record. Provide customizeable help overlay. Support generating printable family group records with optional source citations and relationship charts. Operate as sovereign local-first module.

Create a bounded TextDimensionService module that measures rendered text dimensions (width/height) based on font specifications and returns pixel measurements for UI layout decisions. The module should support: font family, size, weight, and style parameters; output width and height in pixels; caching for performance; truncation detection; and adapters for common UI frameworks.

Create a personal genealogy desktop application that stores family member profiles with structured relationships (parent, child, sibling, spouse), supports attaching media to person records, generates multi-generational pedigree and descendancy charts (2-12 generations), produces formatted kinship reports including second-cousin calculations, runs data integrity checks, and exports reports to standard formats. All data stored locally in SQLite with no cloud dependency.
User's age, sex, lifestyle habits (sleep, exercise, diet, stress), biometric indicators, family history, environmental factors

Build a bounded shift-swap calendar module where employees can view shared schedules, request shift trades with colleagues, and managers can approve or reject swaps—with approved trades automatically recording onto the shared calendar and syncing to personal calendars.

Build a kid-friendly holiday scene decorator web module with a fixed Christmas tree background. Include a palette of draggable decorations (ornaments, stars, lights, presents, candy canes). Users can drag decorations onto the tree, reposition them by clicking and dragging. Add a simple 'Save Picture' button that exports the decorated scene as a PNG file. Keep it self-contained, local-first, with no user accounts or cloud features. Optimized for desktop and tablet use.

Create a local-first meal planning application that allows users to import and manage personal recipes, filter out disliked items, automatically schedule meals for a user-specified number of days while preventing the same meal from appearing on consecutive days, and generate a consolidated shopping list from the scheduled meals that can be printed or exported.

I want to perform a tarot reading with custom layouts, get interpretations tied to each card position, access reference material about card meanings and history, and keep a journal of my readings that can export long entries
store a new recipe with title, ingredients list, step-by-step instructions, and one or more category tags

Create a bounded local-first module for managing personal family genealogy records. The module should enable users to: 1) Create and edit person records with biographical fields (name, birth date, death date, birthplace, occupation, notes). 2) Link person records through parent-child and spouse relationships. 3) Support multiple spouses per person. 4) Support up to fifteen children per family unit. 5) Display a visual lineage/tree view showing generational relationships. 6) Store all data locally in a native desktop format (SQLite or similar). 7) Provide basic search and filtering across the family tree. 8) Export family data in standard genealogy formats (GEDCOM). The interface should be clean, point-and-click, and require no network connection for core functionality.
Reflective journaling session

I need to manage my personal medical reference library. I have collected medical journal articles, clinical guidelines, textbook excerpts, and research notes over many years. I need a local tool to catalog these references with proper citation metadata, organize them by medical specialty and topic, search and retrieve them quickly when I need to reference for clinical decisions or research, and maintain the collection over time with updates and new additions.

Given a malware name or observable in Japanese, return matching entries from the local malware knowledge base including: common names (JP/EN), family classification, affected platforms, observable signs, IOCs, and recommended remediation steps.
local recipe card library for everyday home cooking, quick lookup, offline-first, auto-detected cooking timers, portion scaling, no cloud sync required

Build a personal meal rotation planner as a local-first single-module tool. The user wants to: (1) maintain a catalog of meals organized by category tags like 'soups', 'salads', 'main', 'quick', etc. (2) create separate planning lists for different occasions (weeknight dinners, weekend brunch, guests, meal prep). (3) assign meals to specific days on a weekly grid. (4) track variety so they can see if they've eaten something recently. The tool should NOT include: recipe storage, grocery lists, cloud sync, or user accounts. It should work entirely offline, store data locally (JSON or SQLite), and focus purely on planning and rotation logic. Include a simple UI to add/edit meals with tags, create occasion-specific lists, drag meals onto a weekly calendar, and show a 'recently eaten' indicator for each meal.

I want to keep a personal directory of my contacts—friends, family, colleagues—with their names, addresses, phone numbers, emails, and websites. I need to track birthdays and anniversaries, organize them into categories like "Family," "Friends," "Work," add notes about each person, attach photos, and print mailing labels and contact sheets. Everything should stay local on my device.

You are building CiteVault Local, a self-contained desktop reference manager module. The module manages a personal library of bibliographic citations with attached PDF/DOCX documents. Design the core module with local-first architecture using SQLite. Include import of BibTeX, RIS, CSV, and custom formats. Provide metadata editing for standard citation fields (authors, title, year, journal, DOI, URL). Include document attachment storage with full-text indexing. Provide collection/tag organization, marked-items queue for working bibliography, search across metadata and full-text, and export to BibTeX/RIS/formatted citations. What are the key classes, their responsibilities, and the data flow from import through organization to export?

Design a bounded desktop module for a local-first recipe collection manager that allows users to add, categorize, browse, search, import/export in JSON or CSV format, and print individual recipes or generate PDF cookbooks. The module should work entirely offline with a simple help system.
A collection of personal recipes imported from text files, organized in categories you define yourself, with source attribution and personal notes on each recipe
Create an on-demand malware scanner that targets a specific known threat family, runs portable without installation, provides scan status and results view, and executes remediation actions
design a local-first personal recipe database with recipe creation, ingredient management, and meal categorization for a single desktop user

Design a single bounded module called Shared Service Usage Tracker that lets household members log or import their internet/session usage (user, device, start time, end time/duration, optional data volume), automatically calculates per-user and per-device totals, provides a view to compare against ISP billing cycles or data caps, and exports printable summaries or CSV. Scope strictly to usage tracking and reconciliation—exclude network monitoring, parental controls, or bandwidth optimization. Prioritize local-first storage and simple data exchange with router APIs (OpenWrt, DD-WRT, etc.) as an optional data source. Keep the module sovereign and narrowly focused on billing verification and cost allocation between household members.

Create a bounded personal vehicle maintenance tracking module that allows users to: (1) Add and manage one or more vehicles with basic profile info (make, model, year, VIN, license plate); (2) Log current mileage and track odometer readings over time; (3) Set maintenance schedules based on time intervals (months) or mileage intervals (miles/km); (4) Receive notifications or view upcoming service reminders; (5) Record completed services with date, mileage, cost, vendor, and notes; (6) Export or print a service history report. The module should be standalone, local-first, and suitable for individual or small family use. Avoid fleet management features or broad SaaS integrations.

Build a bounded local-first module for creating and visualizing family trees. Support importing GEDCOM files, managing person records with parent-child and spouse relationships, and rendering both pedigree (ancestors) and descendant views. Use a graph data model for relationships and enable local-only storage with export options.
